task.objective: Post-bootstrap milestone 7 (first slice) per ADR-0007: dependency-free glTF 2.0 GLB exporter (merge-by-material primitives, float32 accessors with POSITION bounds, uint32 indices, PBR color materials with season blend, byte-deterministic output), CLI dispatch via preset format 'gltf', GLB verification tests (C++ container/JSON checks plus independent Python struct-level validation), three.js docs promoted to the GLTFLoader path, and an LOD profile comparison render.

outputs: apps/cli: gltf format dispatch on the export command
tests/unit/test_gltf.cpp (container structure, accessor/manifest consistency, byte-determinism, empty-model rejection); integration cli_export_gltf
docs/integrations/three_js.md: GLB as the recommended path

validation.result: 100% tests passed (14 suites) on both presets

all six species export as GLB; independent Python struct/JSON validation passed (header, chunk alignment, accessor bounds, bufferView containment); CoastalOak 101,531 verts/2 primitives, NebulaSporeTree 3 primitives

risks: GLB merges geometry per material: per-node semantic IDs are OBJ-only until runtime metadata sections exist
No tangent accessors or textures yet (image pipeline)
LOD profiles scale branch tessellation only; leaf reduction, shade pruning and impostors are the next 14_LOD stages
GLB writer requires a little-endian host (checked at runtime); big-endian support deferred
